title: betula sibirica
intro: Betula sibirica, also known as the Siberian birch, is a species of birch tree native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, including Siberia and the Himalayas. The bark and leaves of the plant are used in traditional medicine, and it is known for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Betula sibirica are said to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used in tinctures and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
